news.summary: President and CEO of Computer & Communications Industry Association Matt Schruers joins The Lead.
CNN was at the inaugural event of one of the world's biggest tech shows, which casts a spotlight on the region's thriving startup ecosystem.

A new government report warns that advanced Artificial Intelligence systems could pose an "extinction-level threat" to humans, and that the US must intervene. "I think we should be mindful of it," says Ret. Admiral James Stavridis. But he adds, "there have been big inventions in the past - the printing press, electricity, the internet - all of these have been a decried for the possibility of nefarious activity."

Fareed hosts a spirited debate on the House bill that could lead to a US ban on TikTok, with the American Enterprise Institute's Kori Schake and Glen Gerstell, former general counsel for the National Security Agency. They discuss national-security risks the Chinese-owned app might pose given its many American users.
Grammy award-winning artist Ne-Yo joins CNN's Laura Coates to discuss the impact of artificial intelligence on the music industry.

"Human beings had a play-based childhood from time immemorial," says author Jonathan Haidt. What caused teen mental health decline is "between 2010 and 2015, phones, screens come sweeping in...The most important thing that parents can do is delay the age at which their child gets immersed in internet culture."

In London, a mobile phone is stolen every 6 minutes. "If I steal your phone, I'm stealing a thousand dollars," says digital identity expert David Birch. But "If I can get into your bank account, I can steal $100,000. So that's what they really want." So there are important steps to take immediately - including turning off message preview.

Airlines, banks, casinos, package deliveries, and emergency services around the world are recovering today from what could be "the largest tech outage in history." the root cause was not a foreign agent but linked back to a software update issued by a u-s based cybersecurity firm called "Crowd Strike."  Could this have been avoided?

news.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
Minimum wage rises to £12.71 an hour as firms warn of impact
news.summary: Many businesses have said they will have to pass higher wage costs onto customers.

news.title: Estate agents accuse Rightmove of charging excessive fees
news.summary: The online listing portal is now being pursued in a class action, launched on behalf of potentially hundreds of estate agents.

news.title: Energy bill help would be based on household income, Reeves says
news.summary: The chancellor tells the BBC it is "too early" to say exactly who would get help but hinted any support would not arrive until the autumn.

news.title: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
news.summary: The rapper "started feeling very ill" mid-way through the show in New York, her representative told US media.

news.title: 'It's going to be a great show': Crowds gather for countdown to Nasa’s Artemis launch
news.summary: Huge crowds are gathering around the Kennedy Space Centre to see the launch of the historic moon mission.

news.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
news.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was a "steward" of the White House, not the owner.

news.title: Weight-loss jabs will be offered on NHS for people at risk of further heart attacks
news.summary: More than a million people in England will start being offered the anti-obesity jab for better heart health and to avoid strokes.

news.title: Man charged after seven people hit by car in Derby
news.summary: The pedestrians were seriously injured when they were hit by a black Suzuki Swift on Saturday night.

news.title: Some on-air claims about Dominion Voting Systems were false, Fox News acknowledges in statement after deal is announced
Judge in Fox News-Dominion defamation trial: 'The parties have resolved their case'
news.summary: The judge just announced in court that a settlement has been reached in the historic defamation case between Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems.

news.title: White homeowner accused of shooting a Black teen who rang his doorbell turns himself in to face criminal charges
news.summary:
• 'A major part of Ralph died': Aunt of teen shot after ringing wrong doorbell speaks
• 20-year-old woman shot after friend turned into the wrong driveway in upstate New York, officials say

news.title: 'Difficult to say with a straight face': Tapper reacts to Fox News' statement on settlement
news.summary: A settlement has been reached in Dominion Voting Systems' defamation case against Fox News, the judge for the case announced. The network will pay more than $787 million to Dominion, a lawyer for the company said.

news.title: Millions in the US could face massive consequences unless McCarthy can navigate out of a debt trap he set for Biden

• DeSantis goes to Washington, a place he once despised, looking for support to take on Trump
• Opinion: For the GOP to win, it must ditch Trump
• Chris Christie mulling 2024 White House bid
• Analysis: The fire next time has begun burning in Tennessee

news.title: Newly released video shows scene of Jeremy Renner's snowplow accident
news.summary: Newly released body camera footage shows firefighters and sheriff's deputies rushing to help actor Jeremy Renner after a near-fatal snowplow accident in January. The "Avengers" actor broke more than 30 bones and suffered other severe injuries. CNN's Chloe Melas has more.

news.title: Here are the 20 specific Fox broadcasts and tweets Dominion says were defamatory
news.summary:

news.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
news.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

news.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
news.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

news.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
news.summary: This is it.

news.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
news.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

news.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
news.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

news.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
news.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

news.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
news.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

news.title: Retirement contribution limits will rise in 2019
news.summary: Good news retirement savers: The Internal Revenue Service announced cost of living increases to the contribution limits for retirement-related plans in 2019.
